editorial.title: MSCI setback should spur deeper reform
editorial.body: Korea’s latest failure to secure a place on the watch list for inclusion in the MSCI Developed Markets Index is undeniably disappointing. For a cou...
editorial.pullQuote: Korea's latest failure to secure a place on the watch list is undeniably disappointing.
summary: Korea failed again to join MSCI's Developed Markets Index watch list.
The setback reveals structural weaknesses in Korea's capital markets.
Policymakers must pursue deeper reforms to meet international standards.
editorial.stance: Korea should use this setback as a catalyst for meaningful market reforms rather than accepting repeated failures.
editorial.titleTr: MSCI 지수 편입 좌절, 더 깊은 개혁 촉구
summaryTr: 한국이 MSCI 선진시장지수 관찰대상 편입에 또다시 실패했다.
이번 좌절은 한국 자본시장의 구조적 약점을 드러냈다.
정책 당국자들은 국제 기준을 충족하기 위해 더욱 심화된 개혁을 추진해야 한다.

editorial.title: 징집 축소·선택적 모병제… 사회적 숙의부터
editorial.body: (사설) 이재명 대통령이 선택적 모병제를 강조하며 군 복무 방식의 선택권 확대와 첨단과학기술 중심의 군 구조 개편을 추진한다. 모병제 전환은 병역의 공정성, 인구 절벽, 군 인력 확보, 예산 등 다양한 논란과 과제가 있지만, 사회적 공감대와 국민 안심을 바탕으로 신중하게 추진해야 한다. |
summary: 이재명 대통령, 선택적 모병제 추진으로 군 복무 방식 다양화 추구.
병역 공정성·인구절벽·인력 확보 등 다층적 과제 존재.
사회적 공감대 형성 후 신중한 추진 필요.
editorial.pullQuote: 모병제 전환은 병역의 공정성, 인구 절벽, 군 인력 확보, 예산 등 다양한 논란과 과제가 있다.
editorial.stance: 선택적 모병제는 필요하나 국민 합의와 충분한 검토를 통해 신중히 추진해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Downsizing the Draft, Selective Recruitment... Social Consensus Comes First
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung pursues diversification of military service methods through selective volunteer recruitment system.
Multi-layered challenges exist including fairness in military service, population cliff, and securing manpower.
Careful implementation needed after building social consensus.
editorial.bodyTr: President Lee Jae-myung is emphasizing selective volunteer recruitment and pushing for expanded choice in military service methods alongside restructuring the military around cutting-edge science and technology. The shift toward a volunteer system involves various controversies and challenges surrounding fairness in military service, the demographic cliff, securing military personnel, and budgetary concerns. Nevertheless, it must be pursued carefully on the basis of social consensus and public reassurance.

editorial.body: 정치 편향 유튜브서 호언장담 정책실장, 출마 준비하나 김용범 청와대 정책실장이 26일 김어준씨 유튜브에 나와 29일 열리는 대통령 주재 보고회에서 기업들의 투자 계획이 발표될 것이라고 밝혔다. 이 문제는 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스가 광주·전남에 천문학적 규모의 전공
editorial.pullQuote: 김용범 청와대 정책실장이 26일 김어준씨 유튜브에 나와 29일 열리는 대통령 주재 보고회에서 기업들의 투자 계획이 발표될 것이라고 밝혔다.
summary: 청와대 정책실장이 정치 편향 유튜브에 출연해 논란
대통령 보고회서 기업 투자 계획 공개할 것 미리 언급

editorial.titleTr: Op-Ed: Policy Chief's Bold Promises on Politically Skewed YouTube — Preparing for Election Run?
summaryTr: Presidential office policy chief appears on politically biased YouTube channel, sparks controversy
Presidential briefing mentions advance disclosure of corporate investment plans
Questions raised about candidacy preparations amid policy leak controversy
editorial.bodyTr:
Policy Director Makes Bold Claims on Politically Biased YouTube; Preparing for Political Bid

Kim Yong-bum, the presidential office's policy director, appeared on YouTuber Kim Eo-jun's channel on the 26th and announced that companies' investment plans will be unveiled at a presidential briefing scheduled for the 29th. The matter concerns astronomical-scale semiconductor investments by Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix in Gwangju and Jeollanam-do provinces.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] As Special Probes Pile Up Unsolved Cases, Prosecutors Again Dispatched Over 'Lee's Indictment Dismissal
summaryTr: The number of unsolved cases per prosecutor at the Uijeongbu District Prosecutors' Office is on the verge of surpassing 1,000.
The Daejeon District Prosecutors' Office is also noted as a 'bankrupt branch' with over 500 unsolved cases per prosecutor.
As Lee Jun-seok's indictment is being withdrawn, another prosecutor is being dispatched, repeating the vicious cycle.
editorials.bodyTr: A mounting backlog of unresolved cases plagues multiple special investigations, while Lee's appeal dismissal is overturned and prosecutors are reassigned. Prosecutors assigned to the Uijeongbu District Prosecutors' Office and Namyangju Branch are expected to soon exceed 1,000 unresolved cases per prosecutor. Prosecutor An Mi-hyun at the Daejeon District Prosecutors' Office's Cheonan Branch posted on social media under the title "Bankruptcy Branch," noting that unresolved cases per prosecutor have surpassed 500.

editorials.title: [사설] “거리낌 없이 받았다” 김건희 7년형, 국가적 비극이자 망신
editorials.body: 거리낌 없이 받았다 김건희 7년형, 국가적 비극이자 망신 법원이 윤석열 전 대통령의 부인 김건희 여사의 금품 수수 사건 1심 재판에서 김 여사에게 징역 7년을 선고했다. 대통령 부인 지위를 이용해 인사와 사업 청탁을 들어주는 대가로 금품을 받았다는 것이다. 앞서
editorials.pullQuote: 거리낌 없이 받았다 김건희 7년형, 국가적 비극이자 망신
summary: 법원이 김건희 전 대통령 부인에게 징역 7년을 선고했다.
대통령 부인 지위를 이용한 금품 수수 혐의로 유죄 판정했다.
사설은 이를 국가적 비극이자 망신이라 평가했다.

editorials.titleTr: [Opinion] "Accepted Without Hesitation" Kim Keon-hee's 7-Year Sentence: A National Tragedy and Disgrace
summaryTr: The court sentenced former First Lady Kim Keon-hee to seven years in prison.
She was found guilty of accepting bribes and valuables by exploiting her status as First Lady.
Editorials have characterized this as a national tragedy and disgrace.
editorials.bodyTr: The court handed down a 7-year prison sentence to Kim Keon-hee without hesitation—a national tragedy and disgrace. The court has sentenced Kim Keon-hee, wife of former President Yoon Suk-yeol, to seven years in prison in the first trial of her bribery case. She is accused of accepting money and valuables in exchange for granting favors related to personnel decisions and business matters by leveraging her position as the wife of the president. editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.
summary: 6·3 지방선거에서 투표용지 부족으로 극심한 혼란 발생, 선관위 관리 부실 심각
노태악 전 위원장이 배우자 동반 해외출장에 1200만원대 세금 낭비
선관위는 독립성을 방패 삼아 감시를 피해온 후 전면 개혁 없이는 신뢰 회복 불가
editorial.stance: 선관위의 독립성 뒤에 숨은 적폐를 척결하고 예산·채용 감시 시스템을 도입해 민주주의의 기초인 선거 신뢰를 회복해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: The threshold of seniority
editorial.body: Seoul’s transit reform highlights need to redefine aging in a superaged Korea In politics, age thresholds often survive long after the realities that produced them have disappeared. What begins as a practical benchmark can harden into convention and eventually into policy dogma. South Korea’s definition of old age is approaching that stage. The Seoul Metropolitan Council's decision this week to introduce bus fare support for residents aged 70 and over, alongside discussions about raising the free subway ride eligibility age from 65 to 70, is ostensibly a transportation policy. In reality, it is a test case for something much larger. The immediate pressure comes from fiscal reality. Seoul Metro recorded a deficit of 826.8 billion won ($537 million) last year, of which free rides for seniors accounted for 448.8 billion won. Over the past five years, losses linked to the program have exceeded 3 trillion won. The proposed reform seeks to rebalance, rather than simply reduce, support. Under the plan, citizens aged 70 and older would receive subsidized bus rides, helping address a longstanding blind spot for seniors living in neighborhoods without convenient subway access. The city estimates the annual cost at roughly 50 billion to 60 billion won, far below the savings expected from raising the subway eligibility age. Yet the debate extends far beyond transit budgets. The benchmark age of 65 was codified in the Senior Welfare Act in 1981, when life expectancy stood at 66.7 years. Today it has risen to 83.7 years. Koreans aged 65 and older now account for over 20 percent of the population, making the country a “superaged society.” By 2030, that figure is projected to reach 25.5 percent. Social perceptions have changed just as dramatically. Recent polling indicates that nearly six in 10 Koreans support raising the senior age criterion to 70, while surveys show that many citizens now view the onset of old age as beginning around then. For a growing share of people in their late 60s, employment, volunteering and social participation remain routine parts of life. The label “elderly” no longer carries the same practical meaning it did four decades ago. More revealing is that Seoul's proposal has emerged while national reform remains stalled. The need to reconsider the senior age threshold has been discussed since at least 2019, yet successive governments have hesitated to deal with it. Despite years of consultations and expert reviews, little tangible progress has followed. Political caution is understandable given that the issue touches pensions, healthcare, welfare programs and employment policy. But caution has gradually become inertia. In the absence of a national framework, local governments have begun improvising. Daegu has already linked bus subsidies and subway benefits to a phased increase in eligibility ages, with plans to align both systems at 70. Such local experimentation may prove useful, but it cannot substitute for a coherent national strategy. Yet policymakers must not assume that raising the threshold alone solves the problem. South Korea continues to record the highest elderly poverty rate in the OECD. If welfare eligibility ages rise while retirement practices remain largely unchanged, a gap could emerge between the end of stable employment and access to public support. That would merely shift costs from public accounts to household finances. The debate over seniority must be connected to retirement age policies, reemployment opportunities and the design of better welfare programs. It may also require moving beyond universal age-based benefits toward systems that take income and financial need into account. A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era. Seoul's transit proposal offers a glimpse of that overdue discussion. The objective is not to spend less on older citizens but to build institutions that reflect longer lives, changing patterns of work and the realities of a superaged country. editorial.pullQuote: A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era.
summary: Seoul proposes raising senior transit benefits from age 65 to 70, reflecting Korea's longer lifespans and changing social reality.
The 1981 threshold of 65 is outdated: life expectancy now reaches 83.7 years, and most Koreans support the age increase.
National reform stalled for years, forcing local governments to improvise—but coherent policy must address pensions, employment, and welfare gaps.
editorial.stance: Editorial argues outdated age thresholds must be reformed through coordinated national policy, not piecemeal local fixes.
summaryTr: 서울이 고령층 대중교통 혜택을 65세에서 70세로 상향하는 방안을 제안했으며, 이는 한국의 증가한 기대수명과 변화된 사회 현실을 반영한 것이다.
1981년에 정한 65세 기준은 시대에 뒤떨어졌다: 현재 기대수명은 83.7세에 달하며, 대다수 국민이 나이 상향을 지지한다.
국가 차원의 개혁이 수년간 교착 상태에 빠져 있으면서 지자체들이 임시방편을 강구할 수밖에 없었으나, 일관된 정책이 연금·고용·복지 격차를 포괄적으로 해결해야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
서울의 대중교통 개혁은 초고령사회 한국에서 노년의 정의를 재검토해야 할 필요성을 드러낸다

정치에서 연령 기준은 그것을 만들어낸 현실이 사라진 지 오래된 후에도 종종 생존한다. 실용적인 지표로 시작된 것이 관례로 경화되고 결국 정책 교리로 굳어질 수 있다. 한국의 노년층 정의가 그런 단계에 접어들고 있다. 서울시의회가 이번 주 70세 이상 주민을 대상으로 버스 요금 지원을 도입하기로 결정하고, 지하철 무임승차 대상 연령을 65세에서 70세로 상향할 것을 논의하는 것은 겉으로는 대중교통 정책이다. 실제로는 훨씬 더 큰 문제에 대한 시험 사례다.

즉각적인 압박은 재정 현실에서 비롯된다. 서울메트로는 지난해 8268억 원(5억 3700만 달러)의 적자를 기록했으며, 이 중 노인 무임승차가 4488억 원을 차지했다. 지난 5년간 이 프로그램과 관련된 손실은 3조 원을 초과했다. 제안된 개혁은 단순히 지원을 축소하기보다 재균형을 맞추려는 것이다. 계획에 따르면 70세 이상 시민들은 버스 요금 지원을 받게 되며, 지하철 접근이 편리하지 않은 지역에 사는 노인들을 위한 오랫동안 간과된 부분을 해결하는 데 도움이 될 것이다. 서울시는 연간 비용을 약 500억~600억 원으로 추정하고 있으며, 이는 지하철 대상 연령을 상향함으로써 기대되는 절감액보다 훨씬 낮다.

그러나 논쟁은 대중교통 예산을 훨씬 초월한다. 65세라는 기준 연령은 기대수명이 66.7년이던 1981년 노인복지법에 명시되었다. 오늘날 그것은 83.7년으로 상승했다. 65세 이상인 한국인이 이제 전체 인구의 20% 이상을 차지하면서 한국은 '초고령사회'가 되었다. 2030년이 되면 그 비율은 25.5%에 도달할 것으로 예상된다. 사회적 인식은 이에 못지않게 극적으로 변했다. 최근 여론조사에 따르면 약 6명 중 1명의 한국인이 노인 나이 기준을 70세로 상향하는 것을 지지하며, 설문조사는 많은 시민들이 이제 노년기의 시작을 그 정도로 보고 있음을 보여준다. 60대 후반의 성장하는 인구 집단에게 고용, 자원봉사, 사회 참여는 여전히 일상의 일부다. '노인'이라는 꼬리표는 더 이상 40년 전과 같은 실질적인 의미를 갖지 않는다.

더욱 드러나는 것은 서울의 제안이 국가 차원의 개혁이 정체된 가운데 나타났다는 점이다. 노년층 연령 기준을 재고할 필요성은 최소 2019년 이후로 논의되어 왔지만, 연속된 정부들은 이를 다루기를 주저했다. 수년간의 협의와 전문가 검토에도 불구하고 실질적인 진전이 거의 없었다. 연금, 의료, 복지 프로그램, 고용 정책에 닿아 있기 때문에 정치적 신중함은 이해할 만하다. 하지만 신중함은 점차 관성으로 변해갔다. 국가 차원의 틀이 없는 가운데 지방정부들이 자체적으로 해결책을 모색하기 시작했다. 대구는 이미 버스 지원금과 지하철 혜택을 단계적 수령 연령 상향과 연결했으며, 두 체계 모두를 70세에 맞추려는 계획을 가지고 있다. 그러한 지방 실험이 유용할 수 있지만, 일관된 국가 전략을 대체할 수 없다.

그러나 정책 입안자들은 기준 연령을 상향하는 것만으로 문제가 해결된다고 가정해서는 안 된다. 한국은 여전히 OECD에서 가장 높은 노인 빈곤율을 기록하고 있다. 복지 수급 연령이 상향되는 동안 은퇴 관행이 대체로 변하지 않으면, 안정적인 고용의 종료와 공공 지원 접근 사이에 격차가 생길 수 있다. 그것은 단지 공공 계정에서 가계 재정으로 비용을 옮기는 것일 뿐이다. 노년층에 대한 논쟁은 정년 정책, 재고용 기회, 그리고 더 나은 복지 프로그램의 설계와 연결되어야 한다. 또한 보편적인 연령 기반 혜택을 넘어 소득과 재정적 필요를 고려하는 체계로 이동할 필요가 있을 수 있다.

더 오래 사는 사회는 매우 다른 시대로부터 물려받은 정의에 무한정 의존할 수 없다. 서울의 대중교통 제안은 그러한 지연된 논쟁의 일면을 보여준다. 목표는 노인들에게 쓰는 돈을 줄이는 것이 아니라 더 긴 삶, 변화하는 일의 패턴, 초고령사회의 현실을 반영하는 제도를 구축하는 것이다.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: The editorial acknowledges fiscal constraints and efficiency concerns while simultaneously defending robust welfare provision for seniors and criticizing policy delay, showing genuine balance between fiscal responsibility and social support without clear ideological tilt.

editorials.title: The threshold of seniority
editorials.body: Seoul’s transit reform highlights need to redefine aging in a superaged Korea In politics, age thresholds often survive long after the realities that produced them have disappeared. What begins as a practical benchmark can harden into convention and eventually into policy dogma. South Korea’s definition of old age is approaching that stage. The Seoul Metropolitan Council's decision this week to introduce bus fare support for residents aged 70 and over, alongside discussions about raising the free subway ride eligibility age from 65 to 70, is ostensibly a transportation policy. In reality, it is a test case for something much larger. The immediate pressure comes from fiscal reality. Seoul Metro recorded a deficit of 826.8 billion won ($537 million) last year, of which free rides for seniors accounted for 448.8 billion won. Over the past five years, losses linked to the program have exceeded 3 trillion won. The proposed reform seeks to rebalance, rather than simply reduce, support. Under the plan, citizens aged 70 and older would receive subsidized bus rides, helping address a longstanding blind spot for seniors living in neighborhoods without convenient subway access. The city estimates the annual cost at roughly 50 billion to 60 billion won, far below the savings expected from raising the subway eligibility age. Yet the debate extends far beyond transit budgets. The benchmark age of 65 was codified in the Senior Welfare Act in 1981, when life expectancy stood at 66.7 years. Today it has risen to 83.7 years. Koreans aged 65 and older now account for over 20 percent of the population, making the country a “superaged society.” By 2030, that figure is projected to reach 25.5 percent. Social perceptions have changed just as dramatically. Recent polling indicates that nearly six in 10 Koreans support raising the senior age criterion to 70, while surveys show that many citizens now view the onset of old age as beginning around then. For a growing share of people in their late 60s, employment, volunteering and social participation remain routine parts of life. The label “elderly” no longer carries the same practical meaning it did four decades ago. More revealing is that Seoul's proposal has emerged while national reform remains stalled. The need to reconsider the senior age threshold has been discussed since at least 2019, yet successive governments have hesitated to deal with it. Despite years of consultations and expert reviews, little tangible progress has followed. Political caution is understandable given that the issue touches pensions, healthcare, welfare programs and employment policy. But caution has gradually become inertia. In the absence of a national framework, local governments have begun improvising. Daegu has already linked bus subsidies and subway benefits to a phased increase in eligibility ages, with plans to align both systems at 70. Such local experimentation may prove useful, but it cannot substitute for a coherent national strategy. Yet policymakers must not assume that raising the threshold alone solves the problem. South Korea continues to record the highest elderly poverty rate in the OECD. If welfare eligibility ages rise while retirement practices remain largely unchanged, a gap could emerge between the end of stable employment and access to public support. That would merely shift costs from public accounts to household finances. The debate over seniority must be connected to retirement age policies, reemployment opportunities and the design of better welfare programs. It may also require moving beyond universal age-based benefits toward systems that take income and financial need into account. A society that lives longer cannot indefinitely rely on definitions inherited from a very different era. Seoul's transit proposal offers a glimpse of that overdue discussion. The objective is not to spend less on older citizens but to build institutions that reflect longer lives, changing patterns of work and the realities of a superaged country. summary: Seoul proposes raising senior transit benefits from age 65 to 70, reflecting Korea's longer lifespans and changing social reality.
The 1981 threshold of 65 is outdated: life expectancy now reaches 83.7 years, and most Koreans support the age increase.
National reform stalled for years, forcing local governments to improvise—but coherent policy must address pensions, employment, and welfare gaps.
editorials.stance: Editorial argues outdated age thresholds must be reformed through coordinated national policy, not piecemeal local fixes.

editorial.title: [사설] ‘매관매직’ 김건희 징역 7년, 이런 ‘국정 사유화’ 다신 없어야
editorial.body: 김건희 여사가 공직자와 기업인들로부터 3억원 상당의 금품을 받고 인사와 사업 청탁에 영향력을 행사한 혐의로 1심에서 징역 7년을 선고받았다. 공정하고 투명하게 이뤄져야 할 공적 의사결정을 사적 이익을 위한 거래의 대상으로 전락시킨 ‘매관매직’ 혐의에 대해 법원이 단죄한

editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Kim Keon-hee's 7-Year Prison Sentence for Selling Official Positions Signals End to Privatization of State Affairs
summaryTr: Kim Keon-hee has been sentenced to seven years in prison on charges of accepting 300 million won in bribes and soliciting favors in personnel and business matters.
The court convicted her of 'selling offices for profit,' reducing public decision-making to a transaction for private gain.
Power abuse such as the privatization of state affairs must not be allowed to happen again.
editorial.bodyTr: First Lady Kim Keon-hee was sentenced to seven years in prison in the first trial on charges of receiving approximately 300 million won worth of money and valuables from public officials and business figures while exerting influence over personnel decisions and business solicitations. The court has handed down a judgment on charges of "selling offices and positions" — converting public decision-making that should be conducted fairly and transparently into a subject of transaction for private gain.

editorials.body: 더불어민주당이 6·3 지방선거 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’로 총체적 부실을 드러낸 선거관리위원회를 해체하겠다며 헌법 개정 추진을 공식화했다. ‘도저히 고쳐 쓰기 어려울 정도로 선관위가 망가졌다’는 국민 분노를 엄중히 받아들여, ‘헌법에 규정된 독립기관’이라는 지위를 악용하고

summaryTr: The June Business Sentiment Index fell to 97.7, marking the first decline in three months.
The semiconductor boom has failed to spread across other industries.
Companies continue to experience a cooling in their perceived economic conditions.
editorials.bodyTr: The Bank of Korea's Corporate Business Survey Index (CBSI) reveals the harsh reality that actual economic sentiment remains cold despite the semiconductor boom. The June CBSI fell 1.2 points from the previous month to 97.7, returning to a downward trend for the first time in three months. This is evidence that the warmth from the semiconductor and export boom is failing to spread across the broader industrial base.
